visualGoal: Make a HOSAS pilot feel the sliding-baseplate problem in their gut, then show the clamp-arm fix as obvious and buy-with-the-sticks. Real product proof throughout (mount + the sticks that slide); the hero is the mount itself, not procedural art. Comparison-table clarity for Monstertech vs Foxx vs Virpil.

realProductPhotosNeeded.product: Foxx Mount desk kit (beam + device plate)
realProductPhotosNeeded.notes: US-made modular alternative to Monstertech. Text-only in body because no verified image. Needs a real photo before it can appear as a gearpick/shotgrid or before certification if the guide should visually show every mount. Note: Foxx sells primarily direct, not Amazon — prefer official-store imagery/link over an Amazon buy link for this item.

realProductPhotosNeeded.product: Virpil VPC Desk Mount V4 (S or L)
realProductPhotosNeeded.notes: ST-3 steel matched mount for Virpil bases. Text-only until a real photo is sourced. Official product page 403'd our fetcher; also could not confirm a firm USD price. Source both a real image AND a real USD price before this becomes a body visual or before certification.

Referenced in the desk-vs-chair section (~$119). Only the Monstertech TABLE mount has a verified in-house image; the chair mount does not. A distinct chair-mount photo would let the immersive lap-zone option get its own visual instead of leaning on the table-mount image.

editorialCompositePrompts.prompt: Split-feel editorial cover of a dark home space-sim battlestation lit by neon magenta and cyan ambient glow: on one side a bare desktop with an empty product-overlay footprint where a joystick base would sit (motion-blur ghost suggesting a stick sliding sideways), on the other side a rigid empty clamp-arm footprint bolted to the desk edge, cables tidy, no fake branded hardware, no fake UI text, 16:9. Real product overlays composited into the two footprints in post.
editorialCompositePrompts.negativePrompt: fake logos, fake joystick, warped clamp geometry, impossible ergonomics, tiny unreadable text, procedural gradient art, distorted hands

infographicPrompts.title: Why a HOSAS baseplate slides (and a mount doesn't)
content: Desk resists with only friction + weight
Light base loses -> creeps 1mm per hard input
Clamp arm feeds force INTO the desk
Stick stays locked at your set angle
infographicPrompts.style: IgnitionSim command-center chart, large readable text, space neon palette (magenta/cyan on dark), simple force-arrow diagram, real mount photo as the anchor marker for Codex overlay

infographicPrompts.title: Monstertech vs Foxx vs Virpil at a glance
content: Monstertech ~$129 - widest compat, aluminum, quick-release - DEFAULT
Foxx Mount ~$52 beam + ~$35 plate - US-made modular, cheaper - VERIFY COMPAT
Virpil V4 - ST-3 steel, best for Virpil bases - PRICE CHECK CURRENT
All three: a HOSAS needs TWO
infographicPrompts.style: Three-column comparison, product-photo placeholders for Codex overlays, clear price + best-for row, space palette

infographicPrompts.title: Mount it low and canted inward
content: Cant each stick INWARD to wrist neutral
L and R angles are NOT mirror images
Lock hard - a half-tight arm flexes
infographicPrompts.style: Simple top-down + side ergonomic diagram of a seated pilot with two canted sticks, IgnitionSim chart styling, large labels

curatorScenePrompts.prompt: Realistic adult space-sim curator Val Chen seated at a neon-lit battlestation, one hand relaxed on a clamp-mounted joystick dropped just below desk height and canted inward, calm engineer energy, product-overlay space beside the mount, practical not fantasy, no fake UI, 4:5 portrait.
